title: Combinatorial Creativity: A New Frontier in Generalization Abilities
thesis: Combinatorial creativity is a distinct generalization ability — open-ended, evaluated by degrees of novelty/utility. Introduces the ideation-execution gap and a fundamental novelty-utility tradeoff.
memorable: No free lunch: a fundamental novelty-utility tradeoff persists even at scale; the ideation-execution gap is its empirical signature.

title: Can LLMs Generate Novel Research Ideas? A Large-Scale Human Study
thesis: First rigorous head-to-head: 79 NLP experts blind-reviewed 49 ideas each. AI ideas judged statistically more novel (p<0.05) than expert ideas, slightly weaker on feasibility. Recipe: retrieval + over-generate + rerank.
memorable: LLM-generated NLP research ideas are judged more novel than ideas from 100+ expert researchers (p<0.05) but slightly less feasible.

title: The Ideation-Execution Gap: Execution Outcomes of LLM-Generated vs Human Research Ideas
thesis: Follow-up to Si 2024: 43 experts spent 100+ hours each executing an LLM- or human-generated idea. After execution, LLM ideas dropped more than human ideas on every metric (p<0.05); rankings flipped.
key_techniques: expert blinded review of resulting papers
memorable: After 100+ hours of execution per idea, LLM ideas lose more score than human ideas; final rankings flip.

title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with LLMs
thesis: Generalizes CoT to a search tree: LLM proposes/evaluates/expands thoughts with backtracking. Game-of-24: GPT-4 CoT 4% → ToT 74%. Includes Creative Writing task.
memorable: On Game of 24, GPT-4 with CoT solves 4%; with ToT it jumps to 74% — search structure matters more than the model.

title: Graph of Thoughts: Solving Elaborate Problems with LLMs
thesis: Generalizes ToT from a tree to an arbitrary DAG: thoughts can be merged, refined, looped. +62% quality on sorting vs ToT, >31% fewer LLM calls.
memorable: On sort, GoT beats ToT by 62% in quality with 31% less compute — merging dominates branching for combinatorial tasks.

title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
thesis: Single LLM plays generator → critic → refiner in a loop, no training. ~20% absolute improvement across 7 tasks; humans prefer outputs.
memorable: Just looping output → self-critique → revise yields ~20% absolute improvement across 7 tasks with no training.

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
thesis: Agents reflect on task feedback in natural language; future trials condition on these reflections rather than weight updates. 91% pass@1 on HumanEval, beating GPT-4's 80%.
memorable: Reflexion reaches 91% pass@1 on HumanEval, beating raw GPT-4's 80% — verbal reflection substitutes for gradient RL.

title: Improving Factuality and Reasoning through Multiagent Debate
thesis: Multiple LLM instances independently propose, then debate over rounds to reach consensus. Improves reasoning, reduces hallucinations across six benchmarks. Black-box compatible.
memorable: Three GPT-3.5 agents debating beat one GPT-3.5 with CoT on factuality and arithmetic — pluralism scales without retraining.

title: Encouraging Divergent Thinking in LLMs through Multi-Agent Debate
thesis: Names 'Degeneration-of-Thought' (DoT) — once an LLM commits, self-reflection cannot generate novel alternatives. Adversarial debate (affirmative + negative + judge) is the fix.
memorable: Self-reflection alone cannot escape an LLM's first commitment; only adversarial agents force genuine divergent thinking.

title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
thesis: Open-source framework for conversable-agent applications combining LLMs, humans, and tools. Validated across math, coding, QA, OR, online decisions, entertainment.
memorable: Multi-agent conversation generalizes across math, code, OR, and entertainment with the same framework — the abstraction is the value.

title: ChatDev: Communicative Agents for Software Development
thesis: LLM agents play roles (CEO, CTO, programmer, reviewer, tester) and collaborate via a chat chain to deliver software. Introduces 'communicative dehallucination'.
memorable: Role-played LLM teams produced functional software end-to-end.

title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
thesis: Encodes Standardized Operating Procedures (SOPs) into agent prompts to suppress cascading hallucination. Assembly-line PM/architect/engineer/QA decomposition.
memorable: Encoding human SOPs into prompts is what suppresses cascading hallucination across long agent chains.

title: CAMEL: Communicative Agents for 'Mind' Exploration of LLM Society
thesis: Role-playing 'inception prompting' drives autonomous cooperation between two agents (AI user + AI assistant). Generates large datasets to study multi-agent behavior.
memorable: Two role-played LLM agents can plan and complete a task autonomously from a single seed prompt — origin point for role-play agents.

title: Chain-of-Verification Reduces Hallucination in LLMs
thesis: Four-step verification: draft → plan verification questions → answer them independently to avoid bias → produce verified final response. F1 on closed-book QA improves 23% (0.39→0.48).
memorable: Independently answering verification subquestions — without seeing the draft — is what makes CoVe work.

title: Solo Performance Prompting: Multi-Persona Self-Collaboration
authors: Wang, Mao, Wu, Ge, Wei, Ji
thesis: SPP turns a single LLM into multiple task-specific personas that collaborate over turns. Synergy emerges only at GPT-4-class scale.
memorable: Cognitive synergy from multi-persona self-collaboration only emerges in GPT-4 — a capability threshold.

title: ResearchAgent: Iterative Research Idea Generation over Scientific Literature
thesis: Combines an academic graph + entity knowledge store with an LLM ideator and reviewer agents whose criteria are aligned to human judgments. Iteratively generates problem/method/experiments and refines via simulated peer review.
memorable: Aligning the LLM reviewer's criteria to actual human judgments was the key step that made the iterative refinement loop converge on novel-and-valid ideas.

title: Chain of Ideas: Novel Idea Development with LLM Agents
thesis: Builds chains of literature reflecting progressive development of a domain, then asks LLM to predict next link. Three stages: CoI construction → idea generation w/ novelty checks → experiment design. ~$0.50 per idea.
memorable: CoI generates research-quality ideas at ~$0.50 each — performance comparable to humans, costs comparable to a coffee.

title: Nova: Iterative Planning and Search for Novelty/Diversity
thesis: Plans multi-hop external knowledge retrieval and iteratively expands the idea space. Validated by automated and human assessment over 170 seed papers.
memorable: Nova produces 3.4× more unique novel ideas than baseline and 2.5× more top-rated ideas than SOTA — diversity comes from planned retrieval, not temperature.

title: IdeaSynth: Iterative Research Idea Development through Evolving Idea Facets with Literature-Grounded Feedback
thesis: Decomposes idea into facets (problem, solution, evaluation, contribution) on an interactive canvas. Users mutate/recombine; LLM gives literature-grounded feedback per facet. Lab + field studies show greater exploration and more specific ideas.
memorable: Letting users mutate facets in any order produced more exploration AND more specificity simultaneously.

title: Scideator: Human-LLM Scientific Idea Generation Grounded in Research-Paper Facet Recombination
authors: Radensky, Shahid, Fok, Siangliulue, Hope, Weld
thesis: Extracts purpose/mechanism/evaluation facets from seed + analogous papers, recombines via three RAG modules. User study (N=22) shows significant creativity-support gains; novelty classification 13.79% → 89.66% with facet reranking.
memorable: A facet-based novelty checker boosts novelty-classification accuracy from 13.79% to 89.66% — granularity is the trick.

title: LLMs can Realize Combinatorial Creativity: Generating Creative Ideas via LLMs for Scientific Research
thesis: Explicitly grounds an LLM ideator in classical combinatorial creativity theory. Generalization-level retrieval finds cross-domain knowledge at multiple abstraction levels; structured combinatorial process recombines components. +7-10% similarity to real research developments on OAG-Bench.
memorable: Mapping concepts across abstraction levels before recombination is what enables meaningful cross-domain creative leaps in LLMs.

title: Cooking Up Creativity: Enhancing LLM Creativity through Structured Recombination
thesis: LLMs sample at the surface level and miss real creative leaps. Solution: translate to a structured representation, perform cognitively inspired manipulations (substitution, abstraction, blending), translate back. DishCOVER (recipes) beats GPT-4o on novelty/diversity per domain experts.
memorable: Doing the creative move in a structured schema, not in tokens, is what beats GPT-4o on novelty/diversity for a domain it knows extremely well.

title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
authors: Lu, Lu, Lange, Foerster, Clune, Ha
thesis: End-to-end pipeline: ideate → code → run experiments → visualize → write paper → automated peer review. Three ML subfields. <$15 per paper. Some outputs cleared an automated reviewer's acceptance threshold.
memorable: An autonomous LLM agent can produce a full ML paper for under $15 — the bottleneck is no longer cost, it's truthful novelty assessment.

title: SciAgents: Automating Scientific Discovery through Multi-Agent Intelligent Graph Reasoning
thesis: Combines a large ontological knowledge graph (materials science) with multiple LLM agents that propose, critique, refine hypotheses while pulling external data. Surfaces interdisciplinary connections previously considered unrelated.
memorable: Hidden cross-disciplinary connections in materials science emerged only when the multi-agent swarm was grounded in a large ontological KG.

title: Agent Laboratory: LLM Agents as Research Assistants
thesis: Three-stage autonomous pipeline (lit review → experiments → report writing) seeded by human idea, with optional human feedback at each stage. Best with o1-preview; SOTA-comparable code on some tasks; 84% cost reduction vs prior systems.
memorable: Adding human feedback at each stage measurably improves quality — full automation is not Pareto-optimal.

title: Dolphin: Closed-loop Auto-research through Thinking, Practice, and Feedback
thesis: Closed-loop: ideate → implement → debug (using exception-traceback-guided code repair) → analyze → feed results back to next ideation cycle. SOTA-comparable on 3D point classification with continuous self-improvement.
memorable: Conditioning the next ideation round on the previous round's failed traceback closes a meaningful chunk of the ideation-execution gap.

title: IdeaBench: Benchmarking LLMs for Research Idea Generation
thesis: Benchmark of 2,374 influential papers across 8 domains plus 29,408 references. LLMs profiled as domain researchers; two-stage GPT-4o ranking with 'Insight Score'.
memorable: On IdeaBench, LLMs are good at novelty but reliably weak on feasibility — confirming the Si et al. tradeoff at scale.

title: LiveIdeaBench: Evaluating LLMs' Divergent Thinking with Minimal Context
thesis: Single-keyword prompts test pure divergent thinking. 40+ models, 1,180 keywords, 22 domains. Five dimensions: originality, feasibility, fluency, flexibility, clarity.
memorable: QwQ-32B-preview matches Claude-3.7-Sonnet:thinking on idea generation despite a much lower IQ-style benchmark — creativity is a separable axis from general intelligence.

title: SciMuse: Interesting Scientific Idea Generation with KGs and LLMs
thesis: KG from 58M papers + LLM proposes personalized interdisciplinary ideas. 100+ research group leaders rated 4,400+ ideas; rankers can predict 'interestingness'.
memorable: Both an unsupervised LLM ranker and a small supervised model can predict idea-level interestingness — interestingness is learnable.

title: Human Creativity in the Age of LLMs: RCTs on Divergent and Convergent Thinking
thesis: Pre-registered N=1,100 RCT across no-LLM/standard-LLM/coach-LLM conditions on AUT and RAT. LLM users beat controls during assistance; perform worse than controls when working unassisted.
memorable: LLM-assisted users beat controls during assistance, then lose to controls afterward — measurable creativity-skill atrophy.

title: Fluid Transformers and Creative Analogies: LLMs for Cross-Domain Analogical Creativity
thesis: Studies whether LLMs can supply useful cross-domain analogies. Median helpfulness 4/5; reformulates the problem in ~80% of cases. ~25% of analogies rated 'potentially harmful'.
memorable: LLM analogies caused humans to reformulate their problem in ~80% of cases — the value isn't the analogy itself, it's the perspective shift.

title: AI-Augmented Brainwriting: LLMs in Group Ideation
authors: Shaer, Cooper, Mokryn, Kun, Ben Shoshan
thesis: Integrates an LLM into group brainwriting for both divergent and convergent stages. LLM evaluation engine scores ideas on relevance, innovation, insightfulness; calibrated against expert/novice raters.
memorable: An LLM-as-judge calibrated against human experts is good enough to drive the convergent phase of group ideation.

title: Agent Ideate: Product Idea Generation from Patents Using Agentic AI
thesis: Mines patents to generate downstream product ideas using open-source LLMs vs. agentic architectures across CS, NLP, Material Chemistry. Agentic workflows beat standalone LLMs on quality, relevance, novelty.
memorable: Agentic architectures beat standalone LLMs for turning patents into product ideas — the value-add is the workflow, not just the model.
